run_scope.next_rerun_policy: Use these records only after memory and workflow gates pass; do not claim visual improvement from data records alone.

storage_policy.copyright_boundary: Store URLs, short source metadata, paraphrased observations, and generator obligations only; do not store copied video, audio, frames, screenshots, source layouts, transcripts, logos, or source prose.

segment_locator.public_locator: Config 2025 launch announcement and recap sections covering product expansion, design-to-production workflow, and Figma Slides.
segment_locator.derived_only_note: Use derived launch-arc observations only; no raw media, screenshots, product UI, brand marks, or source copy.

frame_or_visual_observations: The commercial launch bundles multiple products, but the story should reduce breadth into a single platform arc before showing individual features.
A design-to-production claim becomes more legible when each product surface is treated as one stage in a workflow, not as a row of equal feature cards.
The public launch context creates a strong need for a first-read headline that explains why the platform expansion matters before any feature labels appear.
spoken_or_text_claim_paraphrases: Paraphrased claim: the platform is expanding so more roles can move from idea to built product inside one design environment.
Paraphrased claim: design quality and craft remain differentiators as more people participate in software creation.
records.derived_design_method: Compress a multi-product platform announcement into a six-slide route: promise, workflow tension, product-system proof, traceability proof, one climax object, and release gate.
native_ppt_code_obligations: Create editable native stage labels for the six-slide route instead of copying launch imagery.
Render product surfaces as original neutral native shapes with traceable source ids, not source UI screenshots.
Reserve the cover for one launch promise plus one visual workflow cue.
workflow_gate_obligations: Every selected launch-arc slide must record source ids and memory seed ids before generation.
The gate must fail if feature breadth is rendered as equal dashboard cards.
The gate must keep source-brand imitation public blocked.
records.anti_copy_boundary: Do not copy Figma visuals, UI, product names as design marks, screenshots, event graphics, source layout, or source prose.
records.qa_probe: At contact-sheet scale, the deck should read as an original platform launch argument before feature names are readable.

segment_locator.public_locator: Figma Slides product/help pages and Config launch product list.
segment_locator.derived_only_note: Use derived workflow observations only; no raw media, source screenshots, interface copies, or source prose.

frame_or_visual_observations: Presentation tooling is positioned as a collaborative workflow surface, so the output deck should show editable structure rather than a static image composition.
A workflow-oriented slide can expose the decision chain as selected memory, selected gate, and generated native object without turning into a text-heavy diagram.
Interactive presentation surfaces suggest a product-theater composition: one large editable surface, sparse surrounding labels, and a visible handoff state.
spoken_or_text_claim_paraphrases: Paraphrased claim: presentation creation is part of a collaborative design workflow, not a separate export-only task.
records.derived_design_method: Represent PPT generation as a visible native-object workflow with a large product surface, a small decision rail, and an explicit handoff gate.
native_ppt_code_obligations: Generate the product theater surface from editable PPT shapes and text.
Expose selected memory and gate ids as small native labels, not as a full report table.
Use an interaction-handoff object on the close slide to show what the user can do next.
workflow_gate_obligations: The gate must fail if the product surface is raster-only.
The gate must require editable native text and shape counts before claiming product integration.
The gate must record source boundary checks for presentation-product references.
records.anti_copy_boundary: Do not copy Figma UI, slide templates, screenshots, icons, product marks, help prose, or layout patterns.
records.qa_probe: The full arm should show a native product workflow surface that is visibly editable and original.

segment_locator.public_locator: Product keynote transcript section around the Order Intents command-line demo and multi-site agent purchase demonstration.
segment_locator.derived_only_note: Use derived demo-pacing observations only; no raw media, video frames, audio, screenshots, or transcript text.

frame_or_visual_observations: The demo creates tension by starting from a command/action, then externalizes invisible agent work as visible progress.
The strongest sequence beat is not a static screenshot; it is the transition from setup to autonomous work to completed purchase state.
A live-demo imperfection can become a credibility beat if the slide system preserves the sequence and does not over-polish the proof.
spoken_or_text_claim_paraphrases: Paraphrased claim: agentic commerce is easier to understand when viewers see the agent perform a concrete purchase workflow.
Paraphrased claim: the valuable shift is completing multiple external actions without leaving the current context.
records.derived_design_method: Turn a product demo into four native sequence states: command, agent route, progress evidence, and resolved state, with the climax reserved for the resolved state.
native_ppt_code_obligations: Draw the demo sequence as editable native state objects with ordered reveal ids.
Use a visible progress path instead of a row of screenshots.
Keep labels short and delay explanation until after the state transition is legible.
workflow_gate_obligations: The gate must require ordered reveal steps for any demo-sequence slide.
The gate must fail if the demo is represented as equal cards without a climax state.
The gate must require a release-boundary note when public transcript/video material is referenced.
records.anti_copy_boundary: Do not copy Stripe video frames, screenshots, transcript text, logos, event graphics, UI, demo products, or source stage composition.
records.qa_probe: At thumbnail scale, the audience should see a sequence building toward one resolved product state.

segment_locator.public_locator: Product keynote transcript sections that combine business-scale context, product launches, and concrete demo outcomes.
segment_locator.derived_only_note: Use derived metric-framing observations only; no raw media, transcript excerpts, screenshots, or source visuals.

frame_or_visual_observations: Metric proof should be staged as one dominant change, not as a dense table of product claims.
A product keynote can combine scale, launch breadth, and demo proof by making the metric the frame and the product state the object.
The proof slide needs a clear route from metric to action to business implication.
spoken_or_text_claim_paraphrases: Paraphrased claim: scale matters because it allows a product platform to create network-level benefits and practical gains.
Paraphrased claim: product launches become credible when business-scale context connects to a specific workflow demonstration.
records.derived_design_method: Convert numeric or business-scale proof into one large native metric object with a routed product implication and a secondary caveat rail.
native_ppt_code_obligations: Use one large editable metric object with controlled line breaks.
Route proof details into small secondary labels or notes instead of visible tables.
Bind the metric object to a climax or proof slide gate before rendering.
workflow_gate_obligations: The gate must fail if more than one primary metric competes for first read.
The gate must enforce a maximum visible-word budget on metric proof slides.
The gate must require a trace field for deleted or routed content.
records.anti_copy_boundary: Do not copy Stripe transcript text, exact stage visuals, source charts, logos, event graphics, or product UI.
records.qa_probe: The proof slide should read as one designed business delta, not a facts-and-figures report page.

segment_locator.public_locator: White space, visual hierarchy, macro/micro spacing, and high-stakes presentation sections.
segment_locator.derived_only_note: Use derived hierarchy and spacing observations only; no raw media, screenshots, diagrams, or source prose.

frame_or_visual_observations: Macro whitespace separates slide regions so the audience can infer hierarchy before reading.
Micro whitespace protects labels and dense proof from turning into texture.
High-stakes presentation design should align every spacing decision with the business action being requested.
spoken_or_text_claim_paraphrases: Paraphrased claim: white space and hierarchy work together to reduce clutter and direct attention.
Paraphrased claim: spacing should clarify the message, not merely decorate the slide.
records.derived_design_method: Define a two-level spacing system: macro canvas zones for story structure and micro spacing tokens for labels, rails, and proof marks.
native_ppt_code_obligations: Encode macro and micro spacing as numeric layout tokens before drawing slide objects.
Use type size, contrast, and placement to create reading order before color accents are applied.
Fail the slide when equal containers consume the canvas without a dominant zone.
workflow_gate_obligations: The gate must require selected spacing tokens before generation.
The gate must check contact-sheet first-read hierarchy.
The gate must block report-like equal-card layouts for proof and climax roles.
records.anti_copy_boundary: Do not copy article diagrams, screenshots, exact prose, source examples, brand marks, or layouts.
records.qa_probe: The slide should pass a squint-level hierarchy check: one dominant zone, one secondary proof path, and readable spacing.

segment_locator.public_locator: PowerPoint whitespace explanation and video tutorial section.
segment_locator.derived_only_note: Use derived PowerPoint-specific whitespace observations only; no raw media, video frames, screenshots, or source prose.

frame_or_visual_observations: Whitespace should be treated as an active layout object with measurable distance from shapes and labels.
A blank region can carry hierarchy if it separates the proof object from the explanatory guide.
PowerPoint-specific implementation matters because the same design rule must become coordinates, gaps, and editable objects.
spoken_or_text_claim_paraphrases: Paraphrased claim: whitespace is not wasted space; it helps transform dense slides into cleaner communication.
Paraphrased claim: a slide does not need to be filled with text and graphics to make its point.
records.derived_design_method: Translate whitespace teaching into native PPT coordinate obligations: minimum margins, guide-to-object gaps, and protected quiet zones.
native_ppt_code_obligations: Set minimum margin and gutter tokens before shape placement.
Record whitespace budget in trace fields through density counts and layout geometry checks.
Render guide objects as optional native helpers, not copied tutorial arrows.
workflow_gate_obligations: The gate must fail if margins collapse below the selected spacing token set.
The gate must require layout geometry checks before internal demo scoring.
The gate must preserve source-boundary language for PowerPoint tutorial references.
records.anti_copy_boundary: Do not copy tutorial video, screenshots, arrows, diagrams, source prose, downloadable assets, or brand marks.
records.qa_probe: The native PPT output should show measurable whitespace as a designed element, not leftover blankness.
